# SuperPlotR

The goal of SuperPlotR is to allow the user to easily generate
SuperPlots in R.

SuperPlots are a way to visualise data in a way that emphasises the
experimental reproducibility. You can read more about SuperPlots in the
[original paper](https://doi.org/10.1083/jcb.202001064):

Lord, S.J., Velle, K.B., Mullins, R.D. & Fritz-Laylin, L.K. (2020)
**SuperPlots: Communicating reproducibility and variability in cell
biology.** *J. Cell Biol.* 219(6):e202001064

## Installation

You can install the development version of SuperPlotR from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("quantixed/SuperPlotR")
```

## Example

A simple example of how to use SuperPlotR is shown below:

``` r
library(SuperPlotR)
superplot(lord_jcb, "Speed", "Treatment", "Replicate", ylab = "Speed (µm/min)")
```

![](reference/figures/README-example-1.png)

The data used in this example is included in the package and is from the
original paper.

To generate a SuperPlot, you need a data frame that has at least three
columns. These columns are:

1.  the variable that is measured in the experiment,
2.  the experimental condition,
3.  the experimental repeat that the measurement comes from.

The
[`superplot()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/superplot.md)
function takes the data frame, the names of these three columns, and is
further customisable with additional arguments.

See
[`vignette("SuperPlotR")`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/articles/SuperPlotR.md)
for more examples, or
[`vignette("advanced")`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/articles/advanced.md)
for more advanced usage.

## More information

My SuperPlot package for IGOR Pro is available
[here](https://github.com/quantixed/SuperPlot)

A popular web-based app for generating SuperPlots, developed by [Joachim
Goedhart](https://github.com/JoachimGoedhart) is available
[here](https://huygens.science.uva.nl/SuperPlotsOfData/)

# Package index

## All functions

- [`add_sp_bars()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/add_sp_bars.md)
  : Add Mean ± Error Bars to a SuperPlot
- [`flatplot()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/flatplot.md)
  : Make a FlatPlot
- [`get_fp_colour()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/get_fp_colour.md)
  [`get_fp_color()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/get_fp_colour.md)
  : FlatPlot Colour Selection (Single Colour)
- [`get_fp_colours()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/get_fp_colours.md)
  [`get_fp_colors()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/get_fp_colours.md)
  : FlatPlot Colour Selection (Multiple Colours)
- [`get_sp_colours()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/get_sp_colours.md)
  [`get_sp_colors()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/get_sp_colours.md)
  : SuperPlot Colour Palette Selection
- [`get_sp_shapes()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/get_sp_shapes.md)
  : Get shapes for data and summary plots
- [`get_sp_stats()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/get_sp_stats.md)
  : Get statistical test results for the plotted data
- [`get_sp_summary()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/get_sp_summary.md)
  : Get summary statistics for SuperPlot data
- [`lord_jcb`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/lord_jcb.md)
  : Example cell migration data
- [`pieplot()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/pieplot.md)
  : Make a pie chart using ggplot2
- [`representative()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/representative.md)
  : Report the representative datapoints in a SuperPlot
- [`setup_rproj()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/setup_rproj.md)
  : Ensure working directory setup
- [`superplot()`](https://quantixed.github.io/SuperPlotR/reference/superplot.md)
  : Make a SuperPlot
